title: dryopteris scabra var. incompleta
intro: Dryopteris scabra var. incompleta, also known as the rough buckler fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities, which are attributed to its high content of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and polyphenols. The health benefits of using Dryopteris scabra var. incompleta include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a tea, tincture, or extract, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
